text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful framework is the SMART criteria, meaning goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” a SMART goal would be “increase page load speed by 15% within three months.” Break the project into smaller milestones, such as completing a prototype or testing a feature, to track progress. Define key deliverables, like reports or software updates, to know exactly what must be produced. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved—this prevents misunderstandings and keeps the team focused on shared objectives.
